questions.q: Which taint effect evicts already-running pods from a node?
questions.explanation: NoExecute both prevents new pods from scheduling AND evicts pods already running on the node if they do not have a matching toleration.

questions.q: A node has the taint env=prod:PreferNoSchedule. A pod has no tolerations. What happens?
options: The pod is never scheduled on that node
The pod may still be scheduled on that node
The pod is scheduled but immediately evicted
questions.explanation: PreferNoSchedule is a soft constraint. The scheduler avoids the node but will use it if no other suitable nodes are available.

questions.q: A toleration guarantees that a pod will be scheduled on a tainted node.
questions.explanation: A toleration allows a pod to be scheduled on a tainted node but does not guarantee it. The scheduler still makes the final placement decision based on all constraints.
